template_vars.question:
Which country has the most time zones, counting its overseas territories?
A) Russia
B) China
C) United States
D) France

Which element has the highest electronegativity on the Pauling scale?
A) Fluorine
B) Oxygen
C) Chlorine
D) Nitrogen

Which planet rotates almost on its side, with an axial tilt of about 98 degrees?
A) Saturn
B) Neptune
C) Uranus
D) Venus

In the human pancreas, which structures secrete insulin?
A) The islets of Langerhans
B) The acinar cells
C) The hepatocytes
D) The adrenal cortex

Who was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize?
A) Irene Joliot-Curie
B) Marie Curie
C) Dorothy Hodgkin
D) Rosalind Franklin

Which novel famously opens with the line "Call me Ishmael"?
A) Moby-Dick
B) Treasure Island
C) The Old Man and the Sea
D) Robinson Crusoe

What is the value of 7 factorial (7!)?
A) 720
B) 5040
C) 2520
D) 40320

What is the approximate speed of light in a vacuum?
A) 300 km/s
B) 30,000 km/s
C) 300,000 km/s
D) 3,000,000 km/s

The Danube River empties into which body of water?
A) The Adriatic Sea
B) The Black Sea
C) The Caspian Sea
D) The Mediterranean Sea

In Western music, how many semitones are there in one octave?
A) 7
B) 8
C) 12
D) 13

What is the atomic number of uranium?
A) 92
B) 90
C) 88
D) 94

In which year was the Magna Carta first sealed?
A) 1066
B) 1215
C) 1295
D) 1348

Which of these African countries was never formally colonized by a European power?
A) Kenya
B) Ghana
C) Morocco
D) Ethiopia

Who composed the opera "The Magic Flute"?
A) Ludwig van Beethoven
B) Richard Wagner
C) Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart
D) Giuseppe Verdi

How many chambers does the heart of a typical fish have?
A) Two
B) Three
C) Four
D) One

What is the value of Euler's number e, rounded to two decimal places?
A) 1.62
B) 3.14
C) 2.62
D) 2.72

Which planet has the longest rotation period (sidereal day) in the Solar System?
A) Mercury
B) Venus
C) Mars
D) Jupiter

Which economist is most associated with the metaphor of "the invisible hand"?
A) Adam Smith
B) John Maynard Keynes
C) David Ricardo
D) Karl Marx

In which country is Mount Kilimanjaro located?
A) Kenya
B) Uganda
C) Ethiopia
D) Tanzania

What is the most abundant metal in Earth's crust?
A) Iron
B) Calcium
C) Aluminium
D) Copper

What is the atomic number of tungsten (W)?
A) 72
B) 74
C) 76
D) 78

In which year did James Chadwick discover the neutron?
A) 1911
B) 1919
C) 1925
D) 1932

In which year did Constantinople fall to the Ottoman Empire?
A) 1204
B) 1389
C) 1453
D) 1492

Which is the closest known star to the Sun?
A) Sirius
B) Barnard's Star
C) Alpha Centauri A
D) Proxima Centauri

A dietary deficiency of thiamine (vitamin B1) causes which disease?
A) Scurvy
B) Beriberi
C) Pellagra
D) Rickets

What is the SI base unit of luminous intensity?
A) Candela
B) Lux
C) Lumen
D) Watt

Which mathematician proved that the real numbers are uncountable using a diagonal argument?
A) Georg Cantor
B) Bernhard Riemann
C) Carl Friedrich Gauss
D) Leonhard Euler

What is the capital of Mongolia?
A) Ulaanbaatar
B) Astana
C) Bishkek
D) Tashkent

The chemical symbol "Sb" stands for which element?
A) Tin
B) Strontium
C) Selenium
D) Antimony

What is the average-case time complexity of the quicksort algorithm?
A) O(n)
B) O(n log n)
C) O(n^2)
D) O(log n)
